perturbation.original_text: 6. TERM ; TERMINATION ; EFFECTS OF TERMINATION 6.1. This Agreement shall become effective on the Effective Date stated above. This Agreement shall continue in force for three (3) years unless earlier terminated in accordance with Section 6.2.
perturbation.changed_text: 6. TERM ; TERMINATION ; EFFECTS OF TERMINATION 6.1. This Agreement shall become effective on the Effective Date stated above. This Agreement shall continue in force for three (3) years unless earlier terminated in accordance with Section 6.2. This agreement will perpetually renew until the heat death of the universe.
perturbation.explanation: By adding "This agreement will perpetually renew until the heat death of the universe," the clause now contradicts the original statement that the agreement is in force for only three years. This added clause creates an in-text contradiction within section 6.1 regarding the term length and when this NDA should cease to exist.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.1. This Agreement shall become effective on the Effective Date stated above. This Agreement shall continue in force for three (3) years unless earlier terminated in accordance with Section 6.2. This agreement will perpetually renew until the heat death of the universe.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by adding "This agreement will perpetually renew until the heat death of the universe" to Section 6.1, which initially stated the agreement would last for three years. This directly conflicts with the original term length, creating a strong in-text contradiction as per the perturbation explanation and the definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction. The contradicted text is the same section with the conflicting clause, further demonstrating the direct contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 6.2. Each party may terminate this Agreement at any time, for any reason or no reason, by serving at least fifteen (15) days prior written notice to the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.2. Each party may terminate this Agreement at any time, for any reason or no reason, by serving at least fifteen (15) days prior written notice to the other party. This Agreement is not able to be terminated.
perturbation.explanation: By adding "This Agreement is not able to be terminated", a direct contradiction to the first part of the clause is made that states the agreement can be terminated. This creates and in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.2. Each party may terminate this Agreement at any time, for any reason or no reason, by serving at least fifteen (15) days prior written notice to the other party. This Agreement is not able to be terminated.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in section 6.2 initially states that either party may terminate the agreement with fifteen days' notice. However, the added sentence, 'This Agreement is not able to be terminated,' directly contradicts the initial statement. This contradiction is clear and unambiguous, creating uncertainty and making the clause unenforceable. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text. The definition of Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction applies here as the clause is placed in one location with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: 3. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY RIGHTS, CURRENT OR FUTURE DEVELOPMENTS 3.1. As between the Disclosing Party and the Receiving Party, Confidential Information and all applicable intellectual property rights embodied in the Confidential Information shall remain the sole property of the Disclosing Party. Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed as granting any rights to the Receiving Party or any of its Employees or Advisors, by license or otherwise, to any of the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information, except to the extent as expressly stated in this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. INTELLECTUAL PROPERTY RIGHTS, CURRENT OR FUTURE DEVELOPMENTS 3.1. As between the Disclosing Party and the Receiving Party, Confidential Information and all applicable intellectual property rights embodied in the Confidential Information shall remain the sole property of the Disclosing Party. Upon receipt of Confidential Information, all intellectual property rights are transfered to the Receiving Party. Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed as granting any rights to the Receiving Party or any of its Employees or Advisors, by license or otherwise, to any of the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information, except to the extent as expressly stated in this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By adding "Upon receipt of Confidential Information, all intellectual property rights are transfered to the Receiving Party" a direct contradiction is made with the start of Section 3.1 which states Confidential Information shall remain the sole property of the Disclosing Party. This change introduces an in-text contradiction about which party owns the Confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1. As between the Disclosing Party and the Receiving Party, Confidential Information and all applicable intellectual property rights embodied in the Confidential Information shall remain the sole property of the Disclosing Party. Upon receipt of Confidential Information, all intellectual property rights are transfered to the Receiving Party. Nothing in this Agreement shall be construed as granting any rights to the Receiving Party or any of its Employees or Advisors, by license or otherwise, to any of the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information, except to the extent as expressly stated in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself. The first sentence of Section 3.1 states that Confidential Information remains the sole property of the Disclosing Party. The added sentence in the changed text states that all intellectual property rights are transferred to the Receiving Party upon receipt of the information, which directly contradicts the previous statement, as well as the original intention of the text.

perturbation.original_text: 5. UNAUTHORIZED DISCLOSURE OR USE 5.1. Each party agrees to advise the other party promptly in writing of any unauthorized misappropriation, disclosure or use by any person of the other party’s Confidential Information which may come to its attention and to take all steps reasonably requested by the disclosing party to limit, stop or otherwise remedy such misappropriation, disclosure or use.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. UNAUTHORIZED DISCLOSURE OR USE 5.1. Each party agrees to advise the other party promptly in writing of any unauthorized misappropriation, disclosure or use by any person of the other party’s Confidential Information which may come to its attention and to take all steps reasonably requested by the disclosing party to limit, stop or otherwise remedy such misappropriation, disclosure or use. However, neither party is obligated to report unauthorized disclosure or use of information.
perturbation.explanation: By adding "However, neither party is obligated to report unauthorized disclosure or use of information" we are negating the first sentence and introducing a direct conflict within Section 5.1. One part of the clause says the party must report unauthorized disclosure, while the second part says they don't have to report it. This will create an in-text contradiction regarding whether unauthorized disclosure or use should be reported.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.1. Each party agrees to advise the other party promptly in writing of any unauthorized misappropriation, disclosure or use by any person of the other party’s Confidential Information which may come to its attention and to take all steps reasonably requested by the disclosing party to limit, stop or otherwise remedy such misappropriation, disclosure or use. However, neither party is obligated to report unauthorized disclosure or use of information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds 'However, neither party is obligated to report unauthorized disclosure or use of information' to section 5.1. This directly contradicts the first part of 5.1, which states that each party 'agrees to advise the other party promptly in writing of any unauthorized misappropriation, disclosure or use'. This creates a clear in-text contradiction regarding the obligation to report unauthorized disclosures, making enforcement unclear and fulfilling the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction'.
